news.title: How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view of the world
news.summary: Pokémon Go was the world’s first augmented-reality megahit. Released in 2016 by the Google spinout Niantic, the AR twist on the juggernaut Pokémon franchise fast became a global phenomenon. From Chicago to Oslo to Enoshima, players hit the streets in the urgent hope of catching a Jigglypuff or a Squ

news.title: Prioritizing energy intelligence for sustainable growth
news.summary: Loudoun County, Virginia, once known for its pastoral scenery and proximity to Washington, DC, has earned a more modern reputation in recent years: The area has the highest concentration of data centers on the planet. news.title: Is the Pentagon allowed to surveil Americans with AI?
news.summary: The ongoing public feud between the Department of Defense and the AI company Anthropic has raised a deep and still unanswered question: Does the law actually allow the US government to conduct mass surveillance on Americans? Surprisingly, the answer is not straightforward. More than a decade after E

news.title: The Download: 10 things that matter in AI, plus Anthropic’s plan to sue the Pentagon
news.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Coming soon: our 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now For years, MIT Technology Review’s newsroom has been ahead of the curve, tracking the dev

news.title: Rakuten fixes issues twice as fast with Codex
news.summary: Rakuten uses Codex, the coding agent from OpenAI, to ship software faster and safer, reducing MTTR 50%, automating CI/CD reviews, and delivering full-stack builds in weeks.

news.title: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
news.summary: How ChatGPT defends against prompt injection and social engineering by constraining risky actions and protecting sensitive data in agent workflows.

news.title: Wayfair boosts catalog accuracy and support speed with OpenAI
news.summary: Wayfair uses OpenAI models to improve ecommerce support and product catalog accuracy, automating ticket triage and enhancing millions of product attributes at scale.

news.title: From model to agent: Equipping the Responses API with a computer environment
news.summary: How OpenAI built an agent runtime using the Responses API, shell tool, and hosted containers to run secure, scalable agents with files, tools, and state.

news.title: Improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs
news.summary: IH-Challenge trains models to prioritize trusted instructions, improving instruction hierarchy, safety steerability, and resistance to prompt injection attacks.

news.title: New ways to learn math and science in ChatGPT
news.summary: ChatGPT introduces interactive visual explanations for math and science, helping students explore formulas, variables, and concepts in real time.

news.title: OpenAI to acquire Promptfoo
news.summary: OpenAI is acquiring Promptfoo, an AI security platform that helps enterprises identify and remediate vulnerabilities in AI systems during development.

news.title: Codex Security: now in research preview
news.summary: Codex Security is an AI application security agent that analyzes project context to detect, validate, and patch complex vulnerabilities with higher confidence and less noise.

news.title: How Descript enables multilingual video dubbing at scale
news.summary: Descript uses OpenAI models to scale multilingual video dubbing, optimizing translations for both meaning and timing so dubbed speech sounds natural across languages.

news.title: How Balyasny Asset Management built an AI research engine for investing
news.summary: See how Balyasny built an AI research system with GPT-5.4, rigorous model evaluation, and agent workflows to transform investment analysis at scale.

news.title: Reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought, and that’s good
news.summary: OpenAI introduces CoT-Control and finds reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought, reinforcing monitorability as an AI safety safeguard.

news.title: Introducing GPT-5.4
news.summary: Introducing GPT-5.4, OpenAI’s most most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work, with state-of-the-art coding, computer use, tool search, and 1M-token context.

news.title: GPT-5.4 Thinking System Card
Ensuring AI use in education leads to opportunity
news.summary: OpenAI shares new tools, certifications, and measurement resources to help schools and universities close AI capability gaps and expand opportunity.

news.title: The five AI value models driving business reinvention
news.summary: Five AI value models show how leaders can sequence AI from workforce fluency to process reinvention and build durable business advantage.

news.title: Chinese brain interface startup Gestala raises $21M just two months after launch
news.summary: This is the largest early-stage funding in China’s brain computer interface industry.

news.title: AI ‘actor’ Tilly Norwood put out the worst song I’ve ever heard
news.summary: This song is an AI actor's rallying cry to other AI actors, urging them to keep going despite the naysayers who doubt their humanity. Literally no one can relate to this.

news.title: Nuro is testing its autonomous vehicle tech on Tokyo’s streets
news.summary: Nuro began testing its self-driving software on public roads in Tokyo, marking the first international expansion for the AV startup.

news.title: Google Play is adding new paid and PC games, game trials, community posts, and more
news.summary: The updates indicate that Google is focused on transforming Google Play into an enhanced gaming hub.

news.title: Ford’s new AI assistant will help fleet owners know if seatbelts are being used
news.summary: Ford Pro AI debuted at Work Truck Week in Indianapolis and is now available to all of its U.S.-based Pro telematics subscribers.

news.title: Ride-hailing inDrive acquires Pakistan’s Krave Mart to bolster grocery delivery
news.summary: inDrive has closed its acquisition of KraveMart, expanding grocery delivery in a market dominated by Foodpanda.

news.title: Zendesk acquires agentic customer service startup Forethought
news.summary: Forethought was years ahead of its time and the 2018 winner of TechCrunch Battlefield.

news.title: India neobank Fi winds down banking services on its platform
news.summary: Fi, founded by former Google Pay executives, is discontinuing banking services over four years after launch.

news.title: Netflix may have paid $600 million for Ben Affleck’s AI startup
news.summary: This deal could rank as among the streaming giant's largest acquisitions ever.

news.title: Lovable says it added $100M in revenue last month alone, with just 146 employees
news.summary: Swedish vibe-coding unicorn Lovable crossed $400 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR) in February.

news.title: Quince hits $10B valuation with giant $500M round led by Iconiq
news.summary: E-commerce company Quince has raised another massive round, less than a year after its previous raise.

news.title: Alternative app store AltStore PAL joins the fediverse
news.summary: AltStore PAL now supports the fediverse, using its own Mastodon server to let iOS developers share app updates and alerts across platforms like Mastodon and Threads.

news.title: Drivers in fatal Ford BlueCruise crashes were likely distracted before impact
news.summary: The National Transportation Safety Board released documents ahead of a March 31 hearing that help show how and why two drivers crashed into stationary vehicles in 2024, leading to three deaths.

news.title: Replit snags $9B valuation 6 months after hitting $3B
news.summary: Replit raised a new $400 million round and said it hopes to have $1B in ARR by year's end.

news.title: Pro-Iran hacktivist group says it is behind attack on medical tech giant Stryker
news.summary: The hacktivist group claimed the attack was in retaliation for a U.S. strike on a Tehran school that killed more than 175 people, most of them children.
